text.title: wireframing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for wireframing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adding color to indicate priority or state in wireframes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Introduces visual design decisions before structural ones are validated, conflating two separate design phases and creating premature commitment to color choices.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates happy-path wireframes only, missing all error and empty states.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicitly add to every wireframe prompt: "include all error, empty, loading, and permission-denied states." Agents do not include these unless explicitly instructed.

failure-mode.description: ASCII wireframes look correct in raw text but break in rendered markdown due to character width differences.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ASCII box-drawing in monospace looks correct in editors but breaks when rendered in GitHub, Notion, or Slack which use proportional fonts. Validate rendering environment before sharing.

failure-mode.description: Annotation tables generated without access to the actual spec contain invented behavior descriptions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always provide the feature spec or acceptance criteria as context when generating annotation tables. Without it, agents invent plausible-sounding behavior that may contradict requirements.

failure-mode.description: Agents omit "open questions" unless explicitly instructed to flag ambiguity.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This is the most valuable part of a wireframe document for early-stage design. Force agents to include open questions with explicit prompt instructions.

failure-mode.description: Human review of wireframes skipped because the agent produced something plausible.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents surface structure but cannot judge whether it matches user mental models or business intent. Human review is mandatory before engineering starts.

failure-mode.description: Presenting one layout option to stakeholders for approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Single-option reviews produce either acceptance without exploration or rejection without alternatives, both of which slow design progress. Minimum three variants before converging.

failure-mode.description: Skipping sketching and opening a Figma file directly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Digital wireframing tools have friction that suppresses exploration. Paper sketching is 10x faster for generating and discarding layout ideas. Open Figma after you know the layout direction.
